source: planning compiler from validated documentation build .codewiki/builds/documentation/2026-05-16-domain-led-layering-refactor-knowledge-codepaths.json

exit_criteria: Implementation tasks map each validated architecture requirement to an owning roadmap task.
Existing active tasks are refined instead of duplicated where they already own the work.
New tasks are atomic enough for implementation: session queue API, domain/application layering, and web UI move are separate.
Next implementation loop starts from planning build and task context, not chat memory.

loop_start.reason: Planning consumed validated documentation build and current roadmap state.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Planning validation should confirm task coverage and no accidental umbrella over-refinement before implementation.

next_loop.reason: Implementation should start fresh from task ids and planning build evidence.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: REQ-ARCH-001 script demotion is implemented through TASK-079 and TASK-081: source-owned audits/gateways/tests must not depend on scripts for semantics.
REQ-ARCH-002 and REQ-ARCH-003 skill-owned bootstrap/prompts/templates are implemented through TASK-083.
REQ-ARCH-004 web UI ownership is implemented through TASK-088.
REQ-ARCH-005, REQ-ARCH-007, REQ-ARCH-008, and REQ-ARCH-009 domain-led source architecture are implemented through TASK-087, with TASK-079/TASK-080/TASK-081 providing audit/gateway enforcement slices.
REQ-ARCH-006 public claim vocabulary removal is implemented through TASK-086 with legacy alias compatibility.

agent_assessment: Planning split the validated architecture into implementation-sized work while preserving existing sprint work. The first create attempt auto-refined umbrella TASK-077 because paths overlapped; TASK-077 was restored to its umbrella scope, then new tasks were created without paths and patched with current implementation paths. This should be validated so implementation does not inherit umbrella pollution.
summary: Planned domain-led architecture implementation from validated documentation: source-owned audit/gateway work, script demotion, skill-owned bootstrap/prompts/templates, session queue scoped lease API, domain/application restructuring, and web UI move.
task_changes: TASK-079 refined: source-owned audit engine; scripts cannot own audit semantics.
TASK-081 refined: move architecture drift checks out of scripts into source-owned audits/tests.
TASK-083 refined: skill-owned bootstrap/prompt/template assets plus dogfood boundary.
TASK-086 created: migrate public coordination API to session queue scoped leases.
TASK-087 created: migrate source to domain-led CodeWiki layers.
TASK-088 created: move web UI out of adapter layer.
TASK-077 restored to original umbrella scope after create auto-reuse/refinement.

TASK-081 refined: move architecture drift checks out of scripts into source-owned audits/tests.
TASK-083 refined: skill-owned bootstrap/prompt/template assets plus dogfood boundary.

tdd_plan: Add or update tests before moving imports: dependency-direction guard, stale public claim terminology guard, frontmatter current-vs-target path guard, UI no-Pi-import guard, and session queue alias behavior tests.
For each migration slice, first add failing test or fixture that captures the intended boundary, then move/wrap code, then update tests/exports/docs.
Keep compatibility exports and aliases until all adapter/package smoke tests pass.

assumptions: TASK-080 remains the active next task for gateway/audit evidence and can consume source-owned audit outcomes from TASK-079/TASK-081 when available.
TASK-087 may need to be implemented in slices or sub-checkpoints because it touches many imports.
open_questions: Whether implementation should start with TASK-081 script demotion/audit substrate before TASK-087 layer moves, or start with TASK-087 wrappers that enable later moves.
Exact public tool name for session queue scoped leases; candidate names include codewiki_session_queue or codewiki_session_lease while codewiki_claim remains alias.
non_goals: Do not move source files during planning.
Do not close any implementation task from planning evidence.
Do not remove legacy compatibility aliases before tests and migration docs exist.
risks: Large layer move can create import churn; use wrapper modules and compatibility exports.
Task creation auto-reused umbrella when target paths overlapped; avoid overloading umbrella tasks in future or add explicit no-reuse create support.
Public tool rename can break existing agents; preserve codewiki_claim alias until migration complete.

validation: codewiki_state refresh after task mutations reports green.
